name: Best Loan Choice
description_short: Maryland-based mortgage lender offering home purchase and refinance loans across the Mid-Atlantic. Founded in 2014, they use wholesale rate shopping and a consultative approach to find competitive rates.
description_long:
Best Loan Choice, Inc. was founded in 2014 by Cory Ruppersberger, a 25-year veteran of the mortgage and banking industry who sought to offer clients better rates and more loan product options than traditional banks. The company operates as a correspondent lender with loan officers based in Baltimore, Maryland and Northern Virginia, serving Maryland, Virginia, Washington D.C., Pennsylvania, Florida, and Alabama.

The company specializes in home purchase and refinance mortgages across multiple loan types: conventional, FHA, VA, USDA, loans for self-employed borrowers, investment properties, and second homes. They participate in first-time homebuyer programs and down payment assistance programs. Best Loan Choice uses proprietary software to shop and compare hundreds of mortgage rates on the wholesale market to identify competitive pricing for clients. They offer free, no-obligation loan consultations and provide secure online pre-approval and application tools accessible from any device.

Best Loan Choice distinguishes itself through a consultative, individualized approach focused on cost analysis and wealth management. The team includes founder Cory Ruppersberger (University of Maryland business school graduate with investment banking securities license and MBNA executive development program background) and loan officers with advanced degrees in business administration and law. They emphasize transparency, handling paperwork and pre-approval letters on behalf of clients, and comparing their rates against hundreds of different banks.

The company's primary limitation is geographic: while they serve multiple states, their physical presence is concentrated in the Baltimore-Washington D.C. corridor. Customer reviews are positive (five-star ratings on Google and Zillow cited), though the website provides limited information about specific loan terms, APR ranges, or fee structures beyond mentions of 'fair fees' and transparency. As a correspondent lender rather than a direct bank, their rate competitiveness depends on wholesale market conditions.
pros: Correspondent lender model provides access to more loan products than traditional banks (conventional, FHA, VA, USDA, self-employed, investment property, second home)
Uses proprietary software to shop and compare hundreds of wholesale mortgage rates in minutes to find competitive pricing
Founder and team have significant credentials: 25+ year industry veteran, business school graduate, investment banking securities license, advanced degrees in business and law
Participates in first-time homebuyer programs and down payment assistance programs for qualifying borrowers
Offers free no-obligation consultations with expert-level total cost analysis and loan comparison
Secure online platform allows pre-approval and application from any computer or mobile device without time constraints
Handles back-and-forth paperwork and pre-approval letters for clients, reducing borrower burden
cons: Limited physical presence—only loan officers in Baltimore and Northern Virginia despite serving 5+ states, which may create accessibility issues for in-person meetings
Website provides no specific information about interest rate ranges, APR examples, or fee schedules to allow upfront comparison shopping
No details about loan processing timelines, closing costs estimates, or specific product requirements (credit score minimums, down payment requirements)
Company size and resources not disclosed; unclear how competitive they are against larger national mortgage lenders
Limited information about customer service availability (business hours, response times for inquiries not listed)
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking down payment assistance programs and expert guidance through the mortgage process
Self-employed borrowers and real estate investors needing non-traditional loan structures
VA and FHA borrowers in the Mid-Atlantic region seeking personalized rate shopping and consultative service
Homeowners in Maryland, Virginia, D.C., Pennsylvania, Florida, or Alabama seeking refinance options with transparent cost analysis
diagnosis: Best Loan Choice is best for borrowers in the Mid-Atlantic region (especially Maryland, Virginia, and D.C.) who value personalized service, expert-level loan consultation, and transparency over the convenience of large national lenders. The main caveat is lack of upfront rate and fee transparency on their website—you must contact them or use their quote form to compare actual pricing, and their limited physical presence may be inconvenient for borrowers seeking in-person branch interaction.
services: Home purchase mortgage loans (conventional, FHA, VA, USDA)
Investment property and second home financing
Free no-obligation loan consultations with cost analysis
Wholesale rate shopping and comparison (hundreds of rates)
Mortgage calculators and cost savings estimators
Complete loan application and documentation handling
Mortgage basics education and learning center resources
meta_description: Maryland-based mortgage lender offering home purchase and refinance loans across the Mid-Atlantic with competitive rates.
